Title: Phenolic acid biosynthesis is associated with deleterious microbiome changes during Plasmodiophora brassicae-induced clubroot in pakchoi

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Allelopathic/root-exuded phenolic acids that suppress beneficial Rhizobiaceae growth and promote clubroot disease progression
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher CCR expression in diseased susceptible roots is associated with increased phenolic acid accumulation and suppression of Rhizobium sp. 25F3-mediated disease resistance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCR transcript level was noticeably elevated in diseased susceptible pakchoi roots (XL_D). 2. Elevated expression coincided with higher rhizosphere p-coumaraldehyde and eudesmic acid levels. 3. These phenolic acids inhibited Rhizobium sp. 25F3 growth and weakened biocontrol against Plasmodiophora brassicae.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression analysis; targeted metabolite quantification; phenolic acid feeding; pathogen biomass qPCR
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in diseased susceptible roots (XL_D) than in healthy susceptible or resistant roots
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plasmodiophora brassicae infection; phenolic acid root drenching
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated with microbiome dysbiosis and enhanced clubroot susceptibility through suppression of beneficial Rhizobium
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for reducing disease-promoting phenolic acid output in roots by editing or downregulating pathogen-induced expression, with caution due to likely roles in phenylpropanoid/lignin defense
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper measured expression correlation but did not provide direct genetic manipulation of CCR; therefore causality is inferred within the phenylalanine metabolic context rather than proven by gene editing or transgenics
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing: CRA018547 (PRJCA029460); non-targeted metabolomics: MTBLS10976

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream general phenylpropanoid entry step linked to p-coumaraldehyde and eudesmic acid formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key entry step; not directly established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Allelopathic phenolic acids that inhibit beneficial Rhizobium sp. 25F3 and promote clubroot disease
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated PAL1/2 expression in susceptible/diseased roots is associated with increased phenolic acid biosynthesis that contributes to microbiome deterioration and higher disease susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL1/2 expression was significantly higher in susceptible varieties (XL_D and XL_H) than in resistant SK_H roots. 2. Increased PAL1/2 expression paralleled higher p-coumaraldehyde and eudesmic acid levels in susceptible rhizospheres. 3. The induced phenolic acids inhibited Rhizobium sp. 25F3 and promoted P. brassicae infection.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression analysis; rhizosphere metabolite profiling and targeted quantification; phenolic acid feeding assay; pathogen biomass qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in susceptible roots, especially under diseased condition
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in XL_D and XL_H roots than SK_H roots
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing PAL activity may lower disease-promoting phenolic acids but could impair broad phenylpropanoid/lignin-related defenses
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to tune root phenolic acid secretion and preserve beneficial microbiota, but likely requires tissue- or stress-specific modulation to avoid compromising defense
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PAL1/2 were assayed as expression markers of the pathway; no direct mutant/transgenic validation was performed in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing: CRA018547 (PRJCA029460); non-targeted metabolomics: MTBLS10976

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core phenylpropanoid conversion toward p-coumarate-derived phenolics
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere phenolic acids associated with inhibition of beneficial Rhizobium and promotion of clubroot
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevated C4H expression in susceptible/diseased roots is associated with increased production of phenolic acid precursors linked to disease-promoting metabolome changes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C4H expression was significantly higher in susceptible varieties than in resistant plants. 2. Elevated C4H accompanied accumulation of p-coumaraldehyde and eudesmic acid in rhizosphere soil. 3. These metabolites suppressed Rhizobium sp. 25F3-mediated clubroot resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression analysis; metabolite quantification; exogenous phenolic acid validation; pathogen biomass qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in susceptible roots under infection context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in XL_D and XL_H roots than SK_H roots
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Editing may alter general phenylpropanoid metabolism and defense-related compounds
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering node for reducing disease-associated phenolic acid accumulation while monitoring impacts on structural and defense phenylpropanoids
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Role inferred from canonical phenylpropanoid pathway and expression analysis in the paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing: CRA018547 (PRJCA029460); non-targeted metabolomics: MTBLS10976

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Activation step toward hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA intermediates leading to p-coumaraldehyde/eudesmic acid-associated phenolics
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phenolic acids that suppress beneficial Rhizobium and facilitate clubroot progression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher 4CL expression in susceptible/diseased roots is associated with enhanced phenolic acid biosynthesis linked to microbiome dysbiosis and disease promotion
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4CL expression was significantly higher in susceptible varieties than in resistant plants. 2. This upregulation coincided with increased rhizosphere accumulation of p-coumaraldehyde and eudesmic acid. 3. The accumulated phenolic acids antagonized Rhizobium sp. 25F3 and enhanced P. brassicae infection.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression analysis; metabolite quantification; exogenous metabolite treatment; pathogen biomass qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in susceptible roots under infection context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in XL_D and XL_H roots than SK_H roots
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may broadly affect lignin, flavonoid, and other phenylpropanoid-derived defenses
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for fine-tuning root-secreted hydroxycinnamate-derived metabolites to maintain beneficial microbiota during clubroot pressure
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper links 4CL expression to phenolic acid accumulation by pathway logic and correlation rather than direct biochemical assay in pakchoi
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing: CRA018547 (PRJCA029460); non-targeted metabolomics: MTBLS10976
